title: Detecting Bill Changes with change_hash
text: Pull raw master list session ’re tracking (find session IDs getSessions) store locally. saveRDS simplest store; database table works way ’re already using one. want full bill detail front — just hashes — can bulk-load session weekly dataset archive instead. getDatasetList shows ’s available getDataset(file = ...) writes ZIP JSON bill records disk. archive costs one query matter many bills session .
code: sessionID <- 2108  baseline <- getMasterListRaw(sessionID = sessionID)  saveRDS(baseline, "bill_hashes.rds") datasets <- getDatasetList(state = "TX")  getDataset(   sessionID = datasets$session_id[1],   accessKey = datasets$access_key[1],   file = "session_archive.zip" )

title: Detecting Bill Changes with change_hash
text: whatever schedule suits (daily typical), fetch master list — one query — join stored hashes. Three cases fall : new bills, changed bills, (rarely) bills dropped list.
code: stored <- readRDS("bill_hashes.rds") current <- getMasterListRaw(sessionID = sessionID)  comparison <- current |>   dplyr::left_join(     stored |> dplyr::select(bill_id, old_hash = change_hash),     by = "bill_id"   )  newBills <- comparison |> dplyr::filter(is.na(old_hash)) changedBills <- comparison |> dplyr::filter(!is.na(old_hash), change_hash != old_hash)  message(nrow(newBills), " new, ", nrow(changedBills), " changed")

title: Detecting Bill Changes with change_hash
text: changed new bill costs one getBill query. quiet day loop nothing . getBill returns full bill record — status, progress, sponsors, votes, document metadata — whatever store downstream can refreshed . also archive bill text, record’s texts element carries doc_id version; pass getText(textID = ..., file = ...) write document disk.
code: toFetch <- c(newBills$bill_id, changedBills$bill_id)  updatedBills <- lapply(toFetch, \(id) getBill(billID = id))

title: Detecting Bill Changes with change_hash
text: Overwrite stored hashes next run diffs today.

title: Detecting Bill Changes with change_hash
text: ’re tracking topic rather session, legiSearchRaw returns bill_id change_hash search results — diff pattern applies. Store hashes last search, re-run search schedule, getBill hits new changed. page search results one query; legiSearchRaw paginates automatically maxPages.

code: @Manual{,   title = {legihelpR: LegiScan R assistant},   author = {Abraham Ruiz},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.3.1},   url = {https://aberuiz.github.io/legihelpR/}, }

title: LegiScan R assistant
text: R package, legihelpR, meant use legiscan API help track legislative actions.

title: LegiScan R assistant
text: can install development version legihelpR GitHub :

title: LegiScan R assistant
text: Requests legiscan require API key function. can register API key legiscan website. can store API key environment using setlegiKey. permanently store legiscan API key environment use argument ‘install = TRUE’. set API key legihelpR functions use getlegiKey check stored environment file default. can use getlegiKey check API key stored. can overwrite existing key using ‘overwrite = TRUE’ setlegiKey.
code: setlegiKey(   APIkey = "< 32 character API Key >",   install = TRUE )

title: LegiScan R assistant
text: Receive dataframe sessions legiscan’s database using getSessions return unique Session IDs provided state.
code: getSessions(state = "MA") #> # A tibble: 9 × 15 #>   session_id state_id state_abbr year_start year_end prefile sine_die prior #>        <int>    <int> <chr>           <int>    <int>   <int>    <int> <int> #> 1       2182       21 MA               2025     2026       0        0     0 #> 2       2026       21 MA               2023     2024       0        1     1 #> 3       1807       21 MA               2021     2022       0        1     1 #> 4       1637       21 MA               2019     2020       0        1     1 #> 5       1413       21 MA               2017     2018       0        1     1 #> 6       1134       21 MA               2015     2016       0        1     1 #> 7       1006       21 MA               2013     2014       0        1     1 #> 8         99       21 MA               2011     2012       0        1     1 #> 9         28       21 MA               2009     2010       0        1     1 #> # ℹ 7 more variables: special <int>, session_tag <chr>, session_title <chr>, #> #   session_name <chr>, dataset_hash <chr>, session_hash <chr>, name <chr>

title: LegiScan R assistant
text: receiving Master List legiscan’s API can use getMasterlist. can provide unique session ID can provide state abbreviation return recent regular session state. track session time without burning API quota, use getMasterListRaw — returns bill’s change_hash can detect bills changed re-fetch getBill. See change detection vignette (vignette("change-detection")) full workflow.

title: LegiScan R assistant
text: Return dataframe search results legiscan using legiSearch function. Reference legiscan’s documentation assistance additional search syntax. can specify dates, status, type, & using legiscan’s search filters ‘query’ argument.
code: legiSearch(   query = "workers compensation",   state = "TX" ) #> 108 Results Found #> # A tibble: 108 × 11 #>    relevance state bill_number bill_id change_hash   url   text_url research_url #>        <int> <chr> <chr>         <int> <chr>         <chr> <chr>    <chr>        #>  1       100 TX    SB1455      1976056 ef4cd00a3f52… http… https:/… https://leg… #>  2        99 TX    HB4483      2002338 e21037c74f19… http… https:/… https://leg… #>  3        99 TX    SB2077      1994907 36b04ee1b8be… http… https:/… https://leg… #>  4        99 TX    HB3914      1993351 b865bd54a9dd… http… https:/… https://leg… #>  5        99 TX    HB4464      2000135 6420227e8d3c… http… https:/… https://leg… #>  6        99 TX    HB5545      2008375 d47eb1a3432f… http… https:/… https://leg… #>  7        99 TX    HB2488      1957337 dae9ad9cd064… http… https:/… https://leg… #>  8        99 TX    HB4067      1994949 c24af9052b24… http… https:/… https://leg… #>  9        98 TX    HB2414      1954268 a8b730069476… http… https:/… https://leg… #> 10        98 TX    HB1667      1897368 f1068ef0e375… http… https:/… https://leg… #> # ℹ 98 more rows #> # ℹ 3 more variables: last_action_date <chr>, last_action <chr>, title <chr>

title: Return search results from legiscan's database — legiSearch
code: legiSearch(   query = NULL,   state = "ALL",   year = 2,   sessionID = NULL,   page = 1,   maxPages = 10,   legiKey = NULL )

title: Return search results from legiscan's database — legiSearch
text: query Enter search text. query may use grammatically correct spacing. Use legiscan's search syntax powerful results. state Search entire nation default '' specify state using letter abbreviations year integer. 1=, 2=Current, 3=Recent, 4=Prior, >1900=Exact Year sessionID Limit search specific session session_id page Default set return Page 1. `legiSearch` paginate include results. maxPages Maximum number pages fetch. page one API query monthly quota. Use `Inf` fetch every page. legiKey 32 character string provided legiscan

title: Return search results from legiscan's database — legiSearch
text: Search results dataframe format

title: Return search results from legiscan's database — legiSearch
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ legiSearch(   query = "action:yesterday AND 'workers compensation'",   state = "TX" )  legiSearch(   query = "intro:month AND wage theft" ) } # }

Use legiscan's search syntax powerful results. state Search entire nation default '' specify state using letter abbreviations year integer. 1=, 2=Current, 3=Recent, 4=Prior, >1900=Exact Year sessionID Limit search specific session session_id page Default set return Page 1. `legiSearchRaw` paginate include results. maxPages Maximum number pages fetch. page one API query monthly quota. Use `Inf` fetch every page. legiKey 32 character string provided legiscan
